code: @Manual{,   title = {droughtevents: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series},   author = {Antonio Jesus Perez-Luque},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/ajpelu/droughtevents}, }

title: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
text: droughtevents provides tools detect, summarize, visualize drought events drought-related index time series (e.g., SPEI, SPI). Given time series threshold, package identifies periods sustained -threshold values, computes summary statistics event (duration, intensity, severity, timing), offers ggplot2-based plotting functions visualize time series highlight detected events.

title: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
text: can install development version droughtevents GitHub :

title: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
text: droughts() identifies drought events time series given index falls specified threshold least min_duration consecutive months (2 default). returned object named list three elements: data: original data, drought flags durations added. drought_events: rows belong detected drought event. drought_assessment: one row per event, duration, intensity, severity, timing.

title: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
text: plot_drought_ts() draws index bar plot, coloring positive (wet, blue) negative (dry, red) periods differently.  can also aggregate year:
code: plot_drought_ts(spei_granada, vname = "spei12", title = "SPEI-12 Time Series") plot_drought_ts(spei_granada, vname = "spei12", by_year = TRUE)

what: Highlight drought events on the plot
title: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
text: add_drought_events() overlays detected events top plot created plot_drought_ts(), shaded bands vertical lines, .
code: p <- plot_drought_ts(spei_granada, vname = "spei12", title = "SPEI-12 Time Series")  p |>   add_drought_events(     drought_assessment = result$drought_assessment,     which_events = "top",     metric = "severity",     top_n = 5,     type = "both",     show_severity = FALSE   )

title: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
text: package ships spei_granada, monthly SPEI time series (6, 12, 24, 48-month scales) Granada, Spain, covering 1901-2024. See ?spei_granada details data source.

title: Identify and Characterize Drought Events from Drought Index Time Series
text: Vicente-Serrano, S.M., Beguería, S., López-Moreno, J.. (2010). Multiscalar Drought Index Sensitive Global Warming: Standardized Precipitation Evapotranspiration Index. Journal Climate, 23(7), 1696-1718. https://doi.org/10.1175/2009JCLI2909.1

what: Add drought period (bands or markers) to a spei time series plot — add_drought_events
title: Add drought period (bands or markers) to a spei time series plot — add_drought_events
text: Adds shaded polygons, vertical lines, ggplot object highlight drought events, based drought assessment summary (returned droughts()). Optionally, labels points can added indicate drought severity.

title: Add drought period (bands or markers) to a spei time series plot — add_drought_events
code: add_drought_events(   p,   drought_assessment,   which_events = c("all", "top"),   metric = c("duration", "severity", "intensity", "lowest_index"),   top_n = 5,   type = c("line", "polygon", "both"),   line_col = "black",   line_type = "dashed",   pol_fill = "gray70",   pol_alpha = 0.5,   show_severity = FALSE )

title: Add drought period (bands or markers) to a spei time series plot — add_drought_events
text: p ggplot object created plot_drought_ts(). drought_assessment data frame columns minyear, month_peak, d_duration, d_severity, others, returned droughts(). which_events Display "" droughts "top" events. metric which_events = "top", use column rank events. Options: "duration", "severity", "intensity", "lowest_index". top_n Number top events display which_events = "top". Default 5. type Type marker display: "polygon" (shaded bands), "line" (vertical lines peak), "". line_col Color vertical line (used). line_type Line type vertical line (e.g. "dashed", "solid"). pol_fill Fill color polygon (used). pol_alpha Transparency polygon fill. show_severity Logical. TRUE, plots point label severity top event.

title: Add drought period (bands or markers) to a spei time series plot — add_drought_events
text: modified ggplot object.

what: Detect and summarize drought events from SPEI droughts index time series — droughts
title: Detect and summarize drought events from SPEI droughts index time series — droughts
text: Identifies drought events time series given index (e.g., SPEI) falls specified threshold least two consecutive months. Returns processed dataset, detected drought events, summary characteristics.

title: Detect and summarize drought events from SPEI droughts index time series — droughts
Detect and summarize drought events from SPEI droughts index time series — droughts
text: df data.frame tibble containing time series data. Must include either: date column class Date POSIXct, separate year month columns. numeric column specified vname contains drought index values. vname string indicating name numeric column evaluated (e.g., "spei12"). threshold numeric threshold values considered part drought event. min_duration Minimum number consecutive months threshold required event considered drought. Default 2.

title: Detect and summarize drought events from SPEI droughts index time series — droughts
text: named list three elements: data: original data frame drought flags durations. drought_events: rows part drought events (duration ≥ 2). drought_assessment: summary event, including duration, intensity, severity, timing.

title: Detect and summarize drought events from SPEI droughts index time series — droughts
code: data(spei_granada) # Detect droughts in the SPEI-12 time series with a threshold of -1.28 droughts_result <- droughts(spei_granada, vname = "spei12", threshold = -1.28)

what: Plot Drought Index Time Series with Positive/Negative Bars — plot_drought_ts
title: Plot Drought Index Time Series with Positive/Negative Bars — plot_drought_ts
text: Creates bar plot drought-related time series (e.g., SPEI SPI), values zero colored differently. Optionally, time series can aggregated displayed year.

title: Plot Drought Index Time Series with Positive/Negative Bars — plot_drought_ts
code: plot_drought_ts(   df,   vname,   title = NULL,   date_col = "date",   pos_color = "blue",   neg_color = "red",   zero_line_color = "black",   zero_line_linetype = "solid",   by_year = FALSE,   y_axis_title = NULL )

title: Plot Drought Index Time Series with Positive/Negative Bars — plot_drought_ts
text: df data.frame tibble containing time series data. vname string. Name numeric column representing drought index. title optional character string plot title. date_col string. Name column containing date information (default "date"). pos_color Color used positive values (default "blue"). neg_color Color used negative values (default "red"). zero_line_color Color horizontal line zero (default "black"). zero_line_linetype Line type horizontal zero line (default "solid"). by_year Logical. TRUE, aggregates drought index year plots annual means (default FALSE). y_axis_title Optional label y-axis. NULL, name vname used.

title: Plot Drought Index Time Series with Positive/Negative Bars — plot_drought_ts
text: ggplot object representing drought index time series bar plot.

title: Plot Drought Index Time Series with Positive/Negative Bars — plot_drought_ts
text: plot useful visualizing temporal dynamics drought indices, highlighting positive (wet) negative (dry) periods. by_year = TRUE, function averages index per year plots one bar per year.

title: Plot Drought Index Time Series with Positive/Negative Bars — plot_drought_ts
code: data(spei_granada) plot_drought_ts(spei_granada, vname = "spei12", title = "SPEI-12 Time Series")  plot_drought_ts(spei_granada, vname = "spei12", title = "SPEI-12 Annual Mean", by_year = TRUE)

what: SPEI time series for Granada — spei_granada
title: SPEI time series for Granada — spei_granada
text: Standardised Precipitation-Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI) time series Granada, Spain (coordinates -3.75, 37.25), four different time scales (6, 12, 24, 48 months). SPEI multi-scalar drought index combines precipitation evapotranspiration data; negative values indicate drier--average conditions, positive values indicate wetter--average conditions. Note longer time scales (spei12, spei24, spei48) require many months prior data computed, contain NaN values start series.

title: SPEI time series for Granada — spei_granada
SPEI time series for Granada — spei_granada
text: data frame (tibble) 1,483 rows 5 columns: date Date observation (class Date), monthly 1901-06-16 2024-12-16. spei6 SPEI 6-month time scale. spei12 SPEI 12-month time scale. NaN first months series. spei24 SPEI 24-month time scale. NaN first months series. spei48 SPEI 48-month time scale. NaN first months series.

title: SPEI time series for Granada — spei_granada
text: https://spei.csic.es/spei_database/

title: SPEI time series for Granada — spei_granada
text: Vicente-Serrano, S.M., Beguería, S., López-Moreno, J.. (2010). Multi-scalar Drought Index Sensitive Global Warming: Standardized Precipitation Evapotranspiration Index. Journal Climate, 23(7), 1696-1718. doi:10.1175/2009JCLI2909.1

title: SPEI time series for Granada — spei_granada
